salientSummary: RepoScanner implemented as a pure-FS, depth-1 scanner parallelized across roots. Detects work-tree repos (.git dir or gitlink file) and bare repos (HEAD + objects/ signature) without shelling out per directory. Missing roots are logged and skipped. 10 Swift Testing cases green; full suite now 82/82. VAL-REPO-001, VAL-REPO-002, VAL-REPO-005, VAL-REPO-006 fulfilled.
whatWasImplemented: Added Sources/Repo/DiscoveredRepo.swift — struct with url, displayName, rootPath, isBare, Identifiable by url. Added Sources/Repo/RepoScanner.swift — enum namespace with static async scan(roots:) parallelizing per-root enumeration via TaskGroup (preserving caller-provided root order in the flat result), and static isRepo(URL) -> RepoKind? classifier using FileManager only. Added RepoKind enum (.workTree / .bare). Missing/non-directory roots and enumeration errors emit os.Logger errors under subsystem nl.rb2.kite category 'repo' and return empty slices, never throw. Within a root, repos are sorted by displayName case-insensitively for deterministic sidebar ordering. Added Tests/KiteTests/RepoTests/RepoScannerTests.swift covering all mandated cases: simple scan, non-repo exclusion, bare detection, missing root, multi-root grouping, isRepo classification (3 variants), deterministic ordering, and a 50-repo perf smoke (<500ms).
whatWasLeftUndone: No wiring to PersistenceStore or AppModel — explicitly out of scope; M2-repo-sidebar will consume RepoScanner.scan(roots:) from a ViewModel.

commandsRun.observation: Project regenerated; Sources/Repo picks up new files via directory scanning.
82 of 82 tests passed (12 suites) in 19.4s; previous 72 still green, 10 new RepoScanner cases added.
